name: White Holiday Fudge
recipeInstructions:
1. Butter a 9 x 13-inch baking pan and set aside.
2. In a heavy 4-quart saucepan, combine cream, corn syrup, butter, and sugar.
3. Place over medium heat and stir occasionally with a wooden spoon until the mixture comes to a boil.
4. If sugar crystals are present, wash down the sides of the pan with a wet pastry brush.
5. Clip on a candy thermometer and cook to 238°F (115°C) or soft-ball stage.
6. Remove from heat and let stand undisturbed until the thermometer reads 200°F (95°C).
7. Remove the thermometer. With a wooden spoon, beat the mixture until it thickens and lightens in color.
8. Stir in vanilla, nuts, and fruits and mix well.
9. Turn into the prepared pan, pressing the mixture into corners.
10. Refrigerate for 24 hours.
11. Cut into 1-inch squares and serve or dip into tempered chocolate if desired.
